subject: 0.8.0-alpha PR decision and post-release destructive code-change acceptance plan

local_evidence.fact: Branch codex/runtime-acceptance-governance is at c4080674125d7a00b83162434ae7a69b4c3a7952, is pushed, and was clean before this report rewrite.
The 0.8.0-alpha branch changes runtime canary evidence, terminal job/lane interpretation, UTF-8 process decoding, release-impact CI routing, release metadata, docs and artifact verification together.
The pushed 0.8.0-alpha branch CI run succeeded before this report rewrite.
No open GitHub pull request existed for codex/runtime-acceptance-governance at the latest local check; it is not merged or released.

A single real readonly dispatch attempt for the current 0.7.1-alpha host was blocked before job creation because only a prior generation had readiness. No readiness, receipt or cache state was edited to force execution.

The prior canary could reject otherwise successful provider evidence when whole-main-checkout status changed during execution. The 0.8.0-alpha branch adds separate canary-evidence regression coverage.

external_evidence.url: KnowledgeRadar deep route: generic_web_ecology + academic_literature_ecology + github_repository_ecology
external_evidence.fact: The research route required official, implementation and academic evidence and explicitly separates search candidates from extracted evidence.
Git documents linked worktrees, throwaway detached worktrees for experiments, and explicit worktree remove/prune management.
SWE-bench applies generated patches to real repositories and runs repository tests in a reproducible containerized environment.
OpenAI recommends evaluating skills as prompt to captured trace and artifacts to a small check set and comparable score, including outcome, process, style and efficiency checks.
GitHub describes artifact attestations as build provenance that establishes where and how artifacts were built.
OpenAI documents plugins as packaging skills and/or MCP-backed apps and distinguishes iterative local workflows from a stable shared package.
OpenAI documents that Desktop, CLI and IDE extension share MCP configuration for a Codex host and describes host-local MCP server behavior.
SWE-bench Live reports overfitting and contamination risk in static evaluation and uses recent real issues with dedicated reproducible environments.
Repository search found an actively maintained standardized benchmark infrastructure for OpenHands; this is implementation context, not a normative source.

research_limitations: No local canary or worker was rerun during this planning pass. Current provider readiness remains an execution-time fact and must be checked after the released 0.8.0-alpha generation is installed.
The repository search result for OpenHands was not used to make technical guarantees; only official Git, GitHub, OpenAI and SWE-bench texts support the normative design choices.
Artifact provenance, host refresh, provider readiness and successful code-change execution are separate evidence layers. No one layer proves the others.
